findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-bigquery, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Hardcoded API key
findings.description: Hardcoded credential found in source file. This credential is embedded in the code and will be exposed in source control.
findings.remediation: Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to source control.

findings.title: subprocess with shell=True
findings.description: subprocess called with shell=True. This passes the command to /bin/sh, allowing injection via shell metacharacters.

location.snippet: subprocess.run("pkill Xvfb; pkill tint2", shell=True)  # noqa: ASYNC221

findings.remediation: Remove shell=True. Pass the command as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg1', 'arg2']).

findings.title: subprocess with shell=True
findings.description: subprocess called with shell=True. This passes the command to /bin/sh, allowing injection via shell metacharacters.
findings.remediation: Remove shell=True. Pass the command as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg1', 'arg2']).

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: fastmcp, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.remediation: Enable SSL verification. If you need to trust a custom CA, use the ca option instead of disabling verification.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-mongo, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Template literal in exec() call
findings.description: User input concatenated into a shell exec() call via template literal. This allows arbitrary command execution.
findings.remediation: Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a string. Never pass user input directly to shell commands.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-mysql, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Hardcoded API key
findings.description: Hardcoded credential found in source file. This credential is embedded in the code and will be exposed in source control.
findings.remediation: Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to source control.

findings.title: subprocess with shell=True
findings.description: subprocess called with shell=True. This passes the command to /bin/sh, allowing injection via shell metacharacters.

location.snippet: subprocess.run("pkill Xvfb; pkill tint2", shell=True)  # noqa: ASYNC221

findings.remediation: Remove shell=True. Pass the command as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg1', 'arg2']).

findings.title: subprocess with shell=True
findings.description: subprocess called with shell=True. This passes the command to /bin/sh, allowing injection via shell metacharacters.
findings.remediation: Remove shell=True. Pass the command as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg1', 'arg2']).

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: fastmcp, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.remediation: Enable SSL verification. If you need to trust a custom CA, use the ca option instead of disabling verification.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-mongo, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Template literal in exec() call
findings.description: User input concatenated into a shell exec() call via template literal. This allows arbitrary command execution.
findings.remediation: Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a string. Never pass user input directly to shell commands.

findings.title: Template literal in exec() call
findings.description: User input concatenated into a shell exec() call via template literal. This allows arbitrary command execution.
findings.remediation: Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a string. Never pass user input directly to shell commands.

findings.title: Template literal in exec() call
findings.description: User input concatenated into a shell exec() call via template literal. This allows arbitrary command execution.
findings.remediation: Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a string. Never pass user input directly to shell commands.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: desktop-commander, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.title: child_process module import (non-execFile)
findings.description: child_process is imported without using execFile(). exec() and spawn() with shell:true are common injection vectors.
findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.remediation: Prefer execFile() over exec(). Never use { shell: true } with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-mysql, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.

findings.remediation: Restore the authentication check. Never ship code with commented-out security controls.

findings.title: Shell interpreter as MCP server command
findings.description: MCP server runs a shell interpreter directly. This provides maximum attack surface for command injection via tool arguments. (server: mcp-ragdocs, command: node)
findings.remediation: Use a specific application binary as the command, not a shell interpreter. The application should validate all inputs.
